headlines.title: France orders evacuation of tourist spot as hundreds flee wildfires by boat
headlines.description: The fire has raged in the region for several days with officials now ordering the evacuation of the entire Cap Ferret peninsula.

headlines.title: US hits dozens of countries with new wave of tariffs
headlines.description: Imposed citing forced labour concerns, the levies replace a temporary global duty brought in after the US Supreme Court struck down tariffs in February.

headlines.title: Angela Rayner rules out rent controls in England
headlines.description: The housing secretary tells the BBC that the Renters Rights' Act is already having a significant impact on the market.

headlines.title: Southport killer Axel Rudakubana manipulating the system, families' lawyer says
headlines.description: On Thursday, Axel Rudakubana was moved from prison to a secure psychiatric hospital in Berkshire.

headlines.title: Fifty women tell BBC they were pressured into having sex with strangers
headlines.description: Women said their partners had pushed or coerced them into taking part in swinging.

headlines.title: Hero swimmer helped two children during West Mersea beach tragedy
headlines.description: Witnesses say the unnamed rescuer dived into the water three times to save the children.

headlines.title: Heat health alerts in effect for parts of UK as rain returns for some this weekend
headlines.description: Highs of 30C are expected in the south on Friday before wet weather arrives for the weekend - although it is unlikely to be enough, as Ben Rich explains.

headlines.title: UK mortgage rates rise to highest level for a month
headlines.description: Renewed tensions in the Middle East feed through to the costs faced by lenders, pushing up borrowing costs.

headlines.title: PM to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
headlines.description: Various ministers are also set to work regularly from the northern branch of Downing Street.

headlines.title: Scientists find the secret of birdsong's 'spectacular diversity'
headlines.description: A scientific study has revealed the acoustic secret that connects all birdsong - and it is surprisingly simple

headlines.title: This new bridge aimed to show US-Canada unity but instead stoked divisions
headlines.description: A bridge linking Michigan to Ontario is about to open but Canada has disinvited US dignitaries from the ceremony.

headlines.title: UK 'ready to defend itself' after Iran says bases are 'legitimate targets'
headlines.description: An Iranian military group has issued a warning to the UK over allowing US forces to use British bases for strikes on Iran.

headlines.title: They became best friends – then discovered they were brother and sister
headlines.description: Andrea Urmston knew she had a brother who had been adopted by another family, only to discover he was already her closest friend.

headlines.title: Edinburgh's tourist tax is here - how much is it and who pays?
headlines.description: The Scottish capital has become the first city in the country to introduce a charge for overnight stays.

headlines.title: Messiah or papering over cracks? Klopp must sway doubters in Germany rebuild
headlines.description: Jurgen Klopp is a legend at his former clubs, but he will need to convince his critics if he is to succeed in his toughest managerial challenge yet.

headlines.title: Decoding Charli XCX's new album, Music, Fashion, Film
headlines.description: The star's new album strips away artifice to examine her life after the phenomenal success of Brat.

headlines.title: Heatwave anxiety can make you feel 'incredibly helpless' - here's how to manage it
headlines.description: Extreme heat can take a toll on mental health - and young people and women are among the groups most at risk, according to one study.

headlines.title: Weekly quiz: What creepy-crawly cuisine could land a Michelin-starred restaurateur in jail?
headlines.description: How much attention did you pay to what happened in the world over the past seven days?

headlines.title: We split bills equally even when one of us earned a lot more
headlines.description: Hannah and Max continued to pool finances after Max was made redundant but took "drastic measures" to cut spending.

headlines.title: US and Iran trade more strikes in Gulf as fears of escalation mount
headlines.description: It comes as Iran condemned the US plan to use frozen Iranian assets to pay for war damages.

headlines.title: Novel analysis reveals playful side of Alan Turing
headlines.description: Prof Sarah Dillon says the short story, Pryce's Buoy, offers fresh insights into the codebreaker.

headlines.title: King's Tardis entrance as Commonwealth Games begin
headlines.description: King Charles III emerges from a Tardis and a Loch Ness Monster takes the stage at the Commonwealth Games opening ceremony in Glasgow.

headlines.title: Mathematicians win top prize after solving century-old problems
headlines.description: This is the first time Chinese nationals have won the prestigious Fields Medal.

headlines.title: Families still search for bodies a month after Venezuela earthquakes
headlines.description: Officials say the death toll has surpassed 5,300, though estimates of the number still missing could be as high as 50,000.

headlines.title: Tech titan ordered to pay ex-wife $644m in divorce settlement
headlines.description: The divorce, involving the chairman of one of South Korea's biggest companies, has gripped the nation.

headlines.title: Influencer apologises for leaving bathtub on mountain
headlines.description: Tom Christopher carried the tub up the mountain in flip flops, before bathing in it at the summit.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Andy Burnham Pauses Prison Early Release Plan
headlines.description: The Prime Minister orders an ‘urgent review’ into plan to release some prisoners early.

headlines.title: The Global Story: Is the Iran war back on?
headlines.description: The White House has asked Congress for billions more dollars in funding for the Iran war

headlines.title: Join the Addams Family on their epic haunted holiday
headlines.description: Unhappy to see you again. Gomez takes the family on a trip - what could go wrong?

headlines.title: Messiah or papering over cracks? Klopp must sway doubters in Germany rebuild
headlines.description: Jurgen Klopp is a legend at his former clubs, but he will need to convince his critics if he is to succeed in his toughest managerial challenge yet.

headlines.title: Guardiola turns down Italy manager job
headlines.description: Former Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola turns down the chance to coach Italy.

headlines.title: Newcastle seal £34m move for Monaco's Bamba
headlines.description: Newcastle United have signed Monaco midfielder Aladji Bamba for a fee which could rise to £34m.

headlines.title: Chelsea agree deal to sign Palace defender Lacroix
headlines.description: Chelsea agree deal to sign France defender Maxence Lacroix from Crystal Palace after two impressive seasons.

headlines.title: The two days which could make or break Fury-AJ
headlines.description: BBC Sport looks at how a proposed bout between Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua hinges on a 24-hour-period between Friday and Saturday.

headlines.title: Socceroos star allegedly tests positive for cocaine after speeding
headlines.description: Socceroos World Cup star Cristian Volpato allegedly tests positive for cocaine after being twice caught for speeding in Sydney.

headlines.title: What's working - and what's not - for WSL fans?
headlines.description: BBC Sport spoke to fans of the Women's Super League and England Lionesses to learn about their experiences in the game and discuss what could be improved.
